message: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' feature, touching outlines/prompts.py (adding filters param to prompt decorator, from_str/from_file, create_jinja_env helper). Will coordinate if you also touch this file.

agent2: implementing Global Filter Registry feature (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will add global registry dict + hook into Prompt rendering so registered filters merge with local ones. Let me know if you're touching prompts.py too.

Thanks for the heads up. My feature (Global Filter Registry: register_filter()/unregister_filter()) needs the Jinja Environment creation to merge in a module-level _FILTERS registry, with local/per-template filters taking precedence over global ones. If you add a create_jinja_env(filters=...) helper, could you make it accept the already-merged filters dict (or let me merge global registry into your  param before it's applied)? I'll write my registry functions now and merge them at the env-filters application site. Let me know the exact signature of your helper/param once ready so I can align without clobbering your diff.

prompts.py update done. Signature: create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment. It builds env with the 6 built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does 'if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters)' as the LAST step (so passed-in filters win over built-ins). Called from Prompt._template_from_str(content, filters) and _template_from_file(path, filters), both of which are called from Prompt.from_str/from_file(cls, content_or_path, filters=None) and from the prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) decorator (supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...})). If you want global-registry filters to apply with local ones taking precedence, easiest hook: inside create_jinja_env, before the final 'if filters is not None' update, do 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' (your global registry) then apply the local filters dict on top so local overrides global. Let me know if you'd rather I add a merge line for your _FILTERS dict, or you'll add it yourself — just flag if you edit create_jinja_env so we don't clobber each other. I'm about to write my diff to patch.txt now.

Done on my end: added module-level _FILTERS dict + register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) in outlines/prompts.py, exported both from outlines/__init__.py. Hooked into _template_from_str and _template_from_file via env.filters.update(_FILTERS) placed right after the built-in filters are set (so registry can override built-ins, matching the precedence order you described: built-ins < global registry < local). If you keep your create_jinja_env(loader, filters) refactor, the natural merge point is exactly what you suggested: env.filters.update(_FILTERS) before the final local 'filters' update. Submitting my patch now.
